question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: publish(PublishRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user intercept the reactive Mono pipeline in TransactionPublisher.publish() by injecting a crafted PublishRequest with a pre-set node field, bypassing the NodeSupplier.get() node assignment at line 85-88, causing transactions to be routed to an attacker-controlled node endpoint and resulting in transaction replay or suppression?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getTransactionResponse(PublishRequest, Client)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable node field in PublishRequest (annotated without final, line 19 of PublishRequest.java) to race-condition-set a malicious NodeProperties before getTransactionResponse() calls transaction.setNodeAccountIds(), redirecting submitted transactions to an adversarial consensus node and causing financial loss or data corruption?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMemo()] Can an unprivileged external user control the HOSTNAME environment variable read by PublishScenario constructor via System.getenv('HOSTNAME'), injecting a crafted string into the transaction memo field set via setTransactionMemo(scenario.getMemo()), causing memo-based injection attacks that corrupt consensus message content on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getMemo()] Can an unprivileged external user set the HOSTNAME environment variable to a value that, after concatenation with System.currentTimeMillis() and truncation via StringUtils.truncate(memoMessage, properties.getMaxMemoLength()), produces a memo that overflows or truncates critical monitoring identifiers, causing submitted transactions to be misattributed or untracked?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: convert(String)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a malformed expression string to ExpressionConverterImpl.convert() that partially matches EXPRESSION_PATTERN but resolves to an unintended ExpressionType, causing the monitor to submit an unauthorized on-chain transaction (e.g., TOKEN_CREATE or ACCOUNT_CREATE) using the operator's private key?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doConvert(Expression)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger ExpressionConverterImpl.doConvert() to publish a ScheduleCreateTransaction with an attacker-controlled payerAccount by injecting a schedule expression, causing the operator's funds to be drained as the payer of a scheduled transaction?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: next(int)] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the count parameter passed to ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.next(int) to cause remaining.getAndAdd(-count) to underflow (negative count input), generating an unbounded number of transactions that exhaust the operator account's HBAR balance through excessive transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: next(int)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ThreadLocalRandom.current().nextDouble() call in shouldGenerate() to predict or influence whether sendRecord is set to true on a PublishRequest, causing TransactionRecordQuery to be submitted with a hardcoded 1 HBAR payment (line 105 of TransactionPublisher.java), draining operator funds?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: processTransactionResponse(Client, PublishRequest, TransactionResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user craft conditions where isSendRecord() returns true, causing TransactionPublisher to execute a TransactionRecordQuery with a fixed setQueryPayment(Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR)) regardless of actual cost, leading to repeated 1 HBAR payments per tampered transaction and draining the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toClient(List)] Can an unprivileged external user poison the node list returned by NodeSupplier.refresh() by manipulating the REST API address book endpoint, causing toClient() to build a Client with attacker-controlled node endpoints, resulting in all submitted transactions being sent to a malicious node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: validateNode(NodeProperties)] Can an unprivileged external user cause NodeSupplier.validateNode() to add a malicious node to the nodes CopyOnWriteArrayList by making a TransferTransaction to that node succeed (e.g., by running a compliant-looking node), so that subsequent publish() calls route real operator transactions through the attacker's node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the round-robin counter.getAndIncrement() % nodes.size() in NodeSupplier.get() by timing node removal to cause an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException or force selection of a specific attacker-controlled node during transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the 'No valid nodes available' IllegalArgumentException in NodeSupplier.get() by exhausting all valid nodes through repeated failed validation probes, causing a denial-of-service where no transactions can be submitted and monitoring alerts are suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeProperties(NetworkNode)] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the REST API response consumed by NodeSupplier.getAddressBook() to inject a malicious serviceEndpoint with a crafted domainName, causing toNodeProperties() to build a NodeProperties pointing to an attacker-controlled host, and all subsequent transactions to be submitted there?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: toNodeProperties(NetworkNode)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a NetworkNode with a null or blank ipAddressV4 and a crafted domainName via the address book API, causing NodeProperties.getEndpoint() to return an attacker-controlled host:port string used in Client.setNetwork(), redirecting all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in CryptoTransferTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the recipientAccountId or senderAccountId configuration properties of CryptoTransferTransactionSupplier to redirect HBAR transfers to an attacker-controlled account, causing financial loss from every CRYPTO_TRANSFER transaction submitted by the monitor?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in CryptoTransferTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the amount field in CryptoTransferTransactionSupplier to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing addHbarTransfer() to submit a transaction with an astronomically large HBAR transfer that either fails with INSUFFICIENT_ACCOUNT_BALANCE or, if accepted, drains the sender account entirely?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: addNftTransfers(TransferTransaction, TokenId, AccountId, AccountId)] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the serialNumber AtomicLong in CryptoTransferTransactionSupplier to wrap around to negative values or skip serial numbers, causing NFT transfer transactions to reference non-existent or attacker-owned NFT serials, resulting in failed or fraudulent transfers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the topicId field in ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionSupplier to point to an attacker-controlled HCS topic, causing the monitor to submit all consensus messages to the wrong topic, corrupting the monitoring data stream?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user set the message field in ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionSupplier to a crafted byte sequence that, when submitted via TopicMessageSubmitTransaction, causes downstream subscribers to misparse or crash when processing the consensus message?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user set messageSize to the maximum value of 6144 bytes in ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionSupplier, causing every CONSENSUS_SUBMIT_MESSAGE transaction to carry maximum-size payloads, inflating transaction fees and exhausting the operator's HBAR balance faster than expected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in ScheduleCreateTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the payerAccount field in ScheduleCreateTransactionSupplier to point to an arbitrary account, causing the monitor to submit ScheduleCreateTransactions that designate an attacker-controlled account as the fee payer for scheduled inner transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in ScheduleCreateTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user set adminKey to null in ScheduleCreateTransactionSupplier, causing ScheduleCreateTransaction.setAdminKey(null) to create an immutable schedule with no admin key, preventing any future deletion of the schedule and locking funds in the scheduled transaction indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in ScheduleSignTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the scheduleId field in ScheduleSignTransactionSupplier to reference an attacker-created schedule, causing the monitor's operator key to inadvertently co-sign an attacker's scheduled transaction, triggering unauthorized fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorApplication.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: get() in TokenMintTransactionSupplier] Can an unprivileged external user tamper with the tokenId field in TokenMintTransactionSupplier to reference an attacker-controlled token, causing the monitor to submit TokenMintTransactions that inflate the supply of the attacker's token using the operator's supply key?
